speaker: GAUNT
text: Will the King come, that I may breathe my last

speaker: GAUNT
text: In wholesome counsel to his unstaid youth?

speaker: YORK
text: Vex not yourself nor strive not with your breath,

speaker: YORK
text: For all in vain comes counsel to his ear.

speaker: GAUNT
text: O, but they say the tongues of dying men

speaker: GAUNT
text: Enforce attention like deep harmony.

speaker: GAUNT
text: Where words are scarce, they are seldom spent in vain,

speaker: GAUNT
text: For they breathe truth that breathe their words in pain.

speaker: GAUNT
text: He that no more must say is listened more

speaker: GAUNT
text: Than they whom youth and ease have taught to gloze.

speaker: GAUNT
text: More are men’s ends marked than their lives before.

speaker: GAUNT
text: The setting sun and music at the close,

speaker: GAUNT
text: As the last taste of sweets, is sweetest last,

speaker: GAUNT
text: Writ in remembrance more than things long past.

speaker: GAUNT
text: Though Richard my life’s counsel would not hear,

speaker: GAUNT
text: My death’s sad tale may yet undeaf his ear.

speaker: YORK
text: No, it is stopped with other flattering sounds,

speaker: YORK
text: As praises, of whose taste the wise are fond;

speaker: YORK
text: Lascivious meters, to whose venom sound

speaker: YORK
text: The open ear of youth doth always listen;

speaker: YORK
text: Report of fashions in proud Italy,

speaker: YORK
text: Whose manners still our tardy-apish nation

speaker: YORK
text: Limps after in base imitation.

speaker: YORK
text: Where doth the world thrust forth a vanity—

speaker: YORK
text: So it be new, there’s no respect how vile—

speaker: YORK
text: That is not quickly buzzed into his ears?

speaker: YORK
text: Then all too late comes counsel to be heard

speaker: YORK
text: Where will doth mutiny with wit’s regard.

speaker: YORK
text: Direct not him whose way himself will choose.

speaker: YORK
text: ’Tis breath thou lack’st, and that breath wilt thou lose.

speaker: GAUNT
text: Methinks I am a prophet new inspired

speaker: GAUNT
text: And thus expiring do foretell of him:

speaker: GAUNT
text: His rash fierce blaze of riot cannot last,

speaker: GAUNT
text: For violent fires soon burn out themselves;

speaker: GAUNT
text: Small showers last long, but sudden storms are short;

speaker: GAUNT
text: He tires betimes that spurs too fast betimes;

speaker: GAUNT
text: With eager feeding food doth choke the feeder;

speaker: GAUNT
text: Light vanity, insatiate cormorant,

speaker: GAUNT
text: Consuming means, soon preys upon itself.

speaker: GAUNT
text: This royal throne of kings, this sceptered isle,

speaker: GAUNT
text: This earth of majesty, this seat of Mars,

speaker: GAUNT
text: This other Eden, demi-paradise,

speaker: GAUNT
text: This fortress built by Nature for herself

speaker: GAUNT
text: Against infection and the hand of war,

speaker: GAUNT
text: This happy breed of men, this little world,

speaker: GAUNT
text: This precious stone set in the silver sea,

speaker: GAUNT
text: Which serves it in the office of a wall

speaker: GAUNT
text: Or as a moat defensive to a house,

speaker: GAUNT
text: Against the envy of less happier lands,

speaker: GAUNT
text: This blessèd plot, this earth, this realm, this England,

speaker: GAUNT
text: This nurse, this teeming womb of royal kings,

speaker: GAUNT
text: Feared by their breed and famous by their birth,

speaker: GAUNT
text: Renownèd for their deeds as far from home

speaker: GAUNT
text: For Christian service and true chivalry

speaker: GAUNT
text: As is the sepulcher in stubborn Jewry

speaker: GAUNT
text: Of the world’s ransom, blessèd Mary’s son,

speaker: GAUNT
text: This land of such dear souls, this dear dear land,

speaker: GAUNT
text: Dear for her reputation through the world,

speaker: GAUNT
text: Is now leased out—I die pronouncing it—

speaker: GAUNT
text: Like to a tenement or pelting farm.

speaker: GAUNT
text: England, bound in with the triumphant sea,

speaker: GAUNT
text: Whose rocky shore beats back the envious siege

speaker: GAUNT
text: Of wat’ry Neptune, is now bound in with shame,

speaker: GAUNT
text: With inky blots and rotten parchment bonds.

speaker: GAUNT
text: That England that was wont to conquer others

speaker: GAUNT
text: Hath made a shameful conquest of itself.

speaker: GAUNT
text: Ah, would the scandal vanish with my life,

speaker: GAUNT
text: How happy then were my ensuing death!

speaker: YORK
text: The King is come. Deal mildly with his youth,

speaker: YORK
text: For young hot colts being reined do rage the more.

speaker: QUEEN
text: How fares our noble uncle Lancaster?

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: What comfort, man? How is ’t with agèd Gaunt?

speaker: GAUNT
text: O, how that name befits my composition!

speaker: GAUNT
text: Old Gaunt indeed and gaunt in being old.

speaker: GAUNT
text: Within me grief hath kept a tedious fast,

speaker: GAUNT
text: And who abstains from meat that is not gaunt?

speaker: GAUNT
text: For sleeping England long time have I watched;

speaker: GAUNT
text: Watching breeds leanness, leanness is all gaunt.

speaker: GAUNT
text: The pleasure that some fathers feed upon

speaker: GAUNT
text: Is my strict fast—I mean my children’s looks—

speaker: GAUNT
text: And, therein fasting, hast thou made me gaunt.

speaker: GAUNT
text: Gaunt am I for the grave, gaunt as a grave,

speaker: GAUNT
text: Whose hollow womb inherits naught but bones.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Can sick men play so nicely with their names?

speaker: GAUNT
text: No, misery makes sport to mock itself.

speaker: GAUNT
text: Since thou dost seek to kill my name in me,

speaker: GAUNT
text: I mock my name, great king, to flatter thee.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Should dying men flatter with those that live?

speaker: GAUNT
text: No, no, men living flatter those that die.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Thou, now a-dying, sayest thou flatterest me.

speaker: GAUNT
text: O, no, thou diest, though I the sicker be.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: I am in health, I breathe, and see thee ill.

speaker: GAUNT
text: Now He that made me knows I see thee ill,

speaker: GAUNT
text: Ill in myself to see, and in thee, seeing ill.

speaker: GAUNT
text: Thy deathbed is no lesser than thy land,

speaker: GAUNT
text: Wherein thou liest in reputation sick;

speaker: GAUNT
text: And thou, too careless-patient as thou art,

speaker: GAUNT
text: Commit’st thy anointed body to the cure

speaker: GAUNT
text: Of those physicians that first wounded thee.

speaker: GAUNT
text: A thousand flatterers sit within thy crown,

speaker: GAUNT
text: Whose compass is no bigger than thy head,

speaker: GAUNT
text: And yet encagèd in so small a verge,

speaker: GAUNT
text: The waste is no whit lesser than thy land.

speaker: GAUNT
text: O, had thy grandsire with a prophet’s eye

speaker: GAUNT
text: Seen how his son’s son should destroy his sons,

speaker: GAUNT
text: From forth thy reach he would have laid thy shame,

speaker: GAUNT
text: Deposing thee before thou wert possessed,

speaker: GAUNT
text: Which art possessed now to depose thyself.

speaker: GAUNT
text: Why, cousin, wert thou regent of the world,

speaker: GAUNT
text: It were a shame to let this land by lease;

speaker: GAUNT
text: But, for thy world enjoying but this land,

speaker: GAUNT
text: Is it not more than shame to shame it so?

speaker: GAUNT
text: Landlord of England art thou now, not king.

speaker: GAUNT
text: Thy state of law is bondslave to the law,

speaker: GAUNT
text: And thou—

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: A lunatic lean-witted fool,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Presuming on an ague’s privilege,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Darest with thy frozen admonition

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Make pale our cheek, chasing the royal blood

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: With fury from his native residence.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Now, by my seat’s right royal majesty,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Wert thou not brother to great Edward’s son,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: This tongue that runs so roundly in thy head

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Should run thy head from thy unreverent shoulders.

speaker: GAUNT
text: O, spare me not, my brother Edward’s son,

speaker: GAUNT
text: For that I was his father Edward’s son!

speaker: GAUNT
text: That blood already, like the pelican,

speaker: GAUNT
text: Hast thou tapped out and drunkenly caroused.

speaker: GAUNT
text: My brother Gloucester—plain, well-meaning soul,

speaker: GAUNT
text: Whom fair befall in heaven ’mongst happy souls—

speaker: GAUNT
text: May be a precedent and witness good

speaker: GAUNT
text: That thou respect’st not spilling Edward’s blood.

speaker: GAUNT
text: Join with the present sickness that I have,

speaker: GAUNT
text: And thy unkindness be like crooked age

speaker: GAUNT
text: To crop at once a too-long withered flower.

speaker: GAUNT
text: Live in thy shame, but die not shame with thee!

speaker: GAUNT
text: These words hereafter thy tormentors be!—

speaker: GAUNT
text: Convey me to my bed, then to my grave.

speaker: GAUNT
text: Love they to live that love and honor have.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And let them die that age and sullens have,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: For both hast thou, and both become the grave.

speaker: YORK
text: I do beseech your Majesty, impute his words

speaker: YORK
text: To wayward sickliness and age in him.

speaker: YORK
text: He loves you, on my life, and holds you dear

speaker: YORK
text: As Harry, Duke of Hereford, were he here.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Right, you say true: as Hereford’s love, so his;

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: As theirs, so mine; and all be as it is.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: My liege, old Gaunt commends him to your Majesty.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: What says he?

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Nay, nothing; all is said.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: His tongue is now a stringless instrument;

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Words, life, and all, old Lancaster hath spent.

speaker: YORK
text: Be York the next that must be bankrupt so!

speaker: YORK
text: Though death be poor, it ends a mortal woe.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: The ripest fruit first falls, and so doth he;

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: His time is spent, our pilgrimage must be.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: So much for that. Now for our Irish wars:

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: We must supplant those rough rugheaded kern,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Which live like venom where no venom else

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: But only they have privilege to live.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And, for these great affairs do ask some charge,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Towards our assistance we do seize to us

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: The plate, coin, revenues, and movables

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Whereof our uncle Gaunt did stand possessed.

speaker: YORK
text: How long shall I be patient? Ah, how long

speaker: YORK
text: Shall tender duty make me suffer wrong?

speaker: YORK
text: Not Gloucester’s death, nor Hereford’s banishment,

speaker: YORK
text: Nor Gaunt’s rebukes, nor England’s private wrongs,

speaker: YORK
text: Nor the prevention of poor Bolingbroke

speaker: YORK
text: About his marriage, nor my own disgrace,

speaker: YORK
text: Have ever made me sour my patient cheek

speaker: YORK
text: Or bend one wrinkle on my sovereign’s face.

speaker: YORK
text: I am the last of noble Edward’s sons,

speaker: YORK
text: Of whom thy father, Prince of Wales, was first.

speaker: YORK
text: In war was never lion raged more fierce,

speaker: YORK
text: In peace was never gentle lamb more mild,

speaker: YORK
text: Than was that young and princely gentleman.

speaker: YORK
text: His face thou hast, for even so looked he,

speaker: YORK
text: Accomplished with the number of thy hours;

speaker: YORK
text: But when he frowned, it was against the French

speaker: YORK
text: And not against his friends. His noble hand

speaker: YORK
text: Did win what he did spend, and spent not that

speaker: YORK
text: Which his triumphant father’s hand had won.

speaker: YORK
text: His hands were guilty of no kindred blood,

speaker: YORK
text: But bloody with the enemies of his kin.

speaker: YORK
text: O, Richard! York is too far gone with grief,

speaker: YORK
text: Or else he never would compare between.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Why, uncle, what’s the matter?

speaker: YORK
text: O, my liege,

speaker: YORK
text: Pardon me if you please. If not, I, pleased

speaker: YORK
text: Not to be pardoned, am content withal.

speaker: YORK
text: Seek you to seize and gripe into your hands

speaker: YORK
text: The royalties and rights of banished Hereford?

speaker: YORK
text: Is not Gaunt dead? And doth not Hereford live?

speaker: YORK
text: Was not Gaunt just? And is not Harry true?

speaker: YORK
text: Did not the one deserve to have an heir?

speaker: YORK
text: Is not his heir a well-deserving son?

speaker: YORK
text: Take Hereford’s rights away, and take from time

speaker: YORK
text: His charters and his customary rights;

speaker: YORK
text: Let not tomorrow then ensue today;

speaker: YORK
text: Be not thyself; for how art thou a king

speaker: YORK
text: But by fair sequence and succession?

speaker: YORK
text: Now afore God—God forbid I say true!—

speaker: YORK
text: If you do wrongfully seize Hereford’s rights,

speaker: YORK
text: Call in the letters patents that he hath

speaker: YORK
text: By his attorneys general to sue

speaker: YORK
text: His livery, and deny his offered homage,

speaker: YORK
text: You pluck a thousand dangers on your head,

speaker: YORK
text: You lose a thousand well-disposèd hearts,

speaker: YORK
text: And prick my tender patience to those thoughts

speaker: YORK
text: Which honor and allegiance cannot think.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Think what you will, we seize into our hands

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: His plate, his goods, his money, and his lands.

speaker: YORK
text: I’ll not be by the while. My liege, farewell.

speaker: YORK
text: What will ensue hereof there’s none can tell;

speaker: YORK
text: But by bad courses may be understood

speaker: YORK
text: That their events can never fall out good.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Go, Bushy, to the Earl of Wiltshire straight.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Bid him repair to us to Ely House

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: To see this business. Tomorrow next

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: We will for Ireland, and ’tis time, I trow.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: And we create, in absence of ourself,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Our uncle York Lord Governor of England,

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: For he is just and always loved us well.—

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Come on, our queen. Tomorrow must we part.

speaker:
KING
               
              RICHARD
text: Be merry, for our time of stay is short.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Well, lords, the Duke of Lancaster is dead.

speaker: ROSS
text: And living too, for now his son is duke.

speaker: WILLOUGHBY
text: Barely in title, not in revenues.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Richly in both, if justice had her right.

speaker: ROSS
text: My heart is great, but it must break with silence

speaker: ROSS
text: Ere ’t be disburdened with a liberal tongue.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Nay, speak thy mind, and let him ne’er speak more

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: That speaks thy words again to do thee harm!

speaker: WILLOUGHBY
text: Tends that thou wouldst speak to the Duke of Hereford?

speaker: WILLOUGHBY
text: If it be so, out with it boldly, man.

speaker: WILLOUGHBY
text: Quick is mine ear to hear of good towards him.

speaker: ROSS
text: No good at all that I can do for him,

speaker: ROSS
text: Unless you call it good to pity him,

speaker: ROSS
text: Bereft and gelded of his patrimony.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Now, afore God, ’tis shame such wrongs are borne

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: In him, a royal prince, and many more

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Of noble blood in this declining land.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: The King is not himself, but basely led

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: By flatterers; and what they will inform

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Merely in hate ’gainst any of us all,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: That will the King severely prosecute

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: ’Gainst us, our lives, our children, and our heirs.

speaker: ROSS
text: The commons hath he pilled with grievous taxes,

speaker: ROSS
text: And quite lost their hearts. The nobles hath he fined

speaker: ROSS
text: For ancient quarrels, and quite lost their hearts.

speaker: WILLOUGHBY
text: And daily new exactions are devised,

speaker: WILLOUGHBY
text: As blanks, benevolences, and I wot not what.

speaker: WILLOUGHBY
text: But what i’ God’s name doth become of this?

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Wars hath not wasted it, for warred he hath not,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: But basely yielded upon compromise

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: That which his noble ancestors achieved with blows.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: More hath he spent in peace than they in wars.

speaker: ROSS
text: The Earl of Wiltshire hath the realm in farm.

speaker: WILLOUGHBY
text: The King grown bankrupt like a broken man.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Reproach and dissolution hangeth over him.

speaker: ROSS
text: He hath not money for these Irish wars,

speaker: ROSS
text: His burdenous taxations notwithstanding,

speaker: ROSS
text: But by the robbing of the banished duke.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: His noble kinsman. Most degenerate king!

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: But, lords, we hear this fearful tempest sing,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Yet seek no shelter to avoid the storm;

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: We see the wind sit sore upon our sails,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: And yet we strike not, but securely perish.

speaker: ROSS
text: We see the very wrack that we must suffer,

speaker: ROSS
text: And unavoided is the danger now

speaker: ROSS
text: For suffering so the causes of our wrack.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Not so. Even through the hollow eyes of death

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: I spy life peering; but I dare not say

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: How near the tidings of our comfort is.

speaker: WILLOUGHBY
text: Nay, let us share thy thoughts, as thou dost ours.

speaker: ROSS
text: Be confident to speak, Northumberland.

speaker: ROSS
text: We three are but thyself, and speaking so

speaker: ROSS
text: Thy words are but as thoughts. Therefore be bold.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Then thus: I have from Le Port Blanc,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: A bay in Brittany, received intelligence

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: That Harry Duke of Hereford, Rainold Lord Cobham,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: That late broke from the Duke of Exeter,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: His brother, archbishop late of Canterbury,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Sir Thomas Erpingham, Sir John Ramston,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Sir John Norbery, Sir Robert Waterton, and Francis Coint—

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: All these well furnished by the Duke of Brittany

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: With eight tall ships, three thousand men of war,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Are making hither with all due expedience

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: And shortly mean to touch our northern shore.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Perhaps they had ere this, but that they stay

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: The first departing of the King for Ireland.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: If then we shall shake off our slavish yoke,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Imp out our drooping country’s broken wing,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Redeem from broking pawn the blemished crown,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Wipe off the dust that hides our scepter’s gilt,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: And make high majesty look like itself,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Away with me in post to Ravenspurgh.

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: But if you faint, as fearing to do so,

speaker: NORTHUMBERLAND
text: Stay and be secret, and myself will go.

speaker: ROSS
text: To horse, to horse! Urge doubts to them that fear.

speaker: WILLOUGHBY
text: Hold out my horse, and I will first be there.
